name: Jack Jones
abstract: John Allan Jones (January 14, 1938 – October 23, 2024) was an American singer and actor. He was primarily a straight-pop singer (even when he recorded contemporary material) whose forays into jazz were mostly of the big-band/swing music variety.

concerts.label: Carnegie Hall Celebrates the Music of Frank Sinatra
Carnegie Hall Celebrates the Music of Ella Fitzgerald
A Celebration of the Music of Judy Garland
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Tommy Newsom and Ross Tompkins
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...There was five different singers — Vic Damone, Jack Jones, Joe Williams, Diane Carroll and Patti Page. [INTERVIEWEE] What a memory he’s got.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tommy Newsom and Ross Tompkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: I said, “Come on.” So I got him up, and he sang a tune. Then he asked me – he says, “How do you scat?”... The other was Jack Jones.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Smith
about_quotes.evidence: Jack Jones I think doesn’t have a contract... And I mean they’re all, from my standpoint, they’re the best singers around.
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Wormworth
about_quotes.evidence: a pianist named Joe Close who became Jack Jones’ musical director for quite a long time in the 70’s
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Wormworth interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Marini
about_quotes.evidence: hearing the richness of a singer like Ella Fitzgerald or Jack Jones or Red Prysock.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Marini interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 15, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: I knew Jack. A couple – “Hi. How you doing?” [?] said, “Yeah, how you doing?”
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
